writeup: The Buffalo Bills wrapped up their 2026 mandatory minicamp on June 11, capping three days of practice with significant roster moves and notable injury news. The most buzzworthy development was the signing of wide receivers Deven Thompkins and Max Tomczak, with Thompkins earning his deal after an impressive tryout that drew attention from multiple national outlets. On the injury front, starting safety Cole Bishop was sidelined during minicamp, raising questions about his availability for training camp, while cornerback Dorian Strong was waived with a non-football injury designation. New head coach Joe Brady addressed the media following the final practice, and analysts took the opportunity to grade the team's full 2026 offseason, with outside linebacker Bradley Chubb emerging as a notable storyline after expressing motivation to contribute alongside young pass rusher TJ Parker.
